Innovative Engineering Solutions Provides Engineering solutions including product design, development, analysis, fabrication and testing.

| |  | | --- | | [Home](index.php) | | [Engineering Services](#) | | [Test Services](#) | | [Special Test Equipment](special_test_equipment.php) | | [Custom Equipment](custom_equipment.php) | | [Product Development](product_development.php) | | [Company & Facilities](#) | | [Contact Us](contact_us.php) | | [Find Us](find_us.php) |   [ISO 9001:9008 Certification](IESISO2023.pdf) | |  | | --- | | Design, Analysis, Fabrication, Testing Innovative Engineering Solutions Murrieta Facility**I****nnovative** **E****ngineering** **S****olutions** (IES) is a full-service engineering company, providing “innovative solutions” to unique problems.  At IES, our experienced engineering teams will assist you with the design, analysis, fabrication and testing of your product.  Since 1995, Innovative Engineering Solutions has offered these services to a wide variety of customers, including the aerospace, industrial, commercial, government and medical sectors.  The expertise of Innovative Engineering Solution's engineers include cryogenic test services, aerospace fluid systems, design, analysis, and fabrication of special test equipment, fluid flow and thermal analysis support, CAD/CAM support, cryogenic system design, industrial control systems, commercial product development (electronic and mechanical), and medical device manufacturing support and testing.  A brief list of our services include:  [Engineering Services](design.php) Thermal Analysis and Graphic ImagingIES provides design and analysis support of mechanical, electrical and fluid systems, including structural analysis, thermal analysis and fluid flow analysis.  Our engineers excel at computational fluid dynamics and finite element analysis of components and structures.  Over the years we have designed and developed numerous products for our customers, including industrial control systems for a variety of factory processes.  We also support the aerospace industry with both flight and ground support equipment (GSE), while for the medical field we have developed many medical devices, special test equipment, and automated test equipment to assist with product development and FDA approval. [Test Services](test_facilities.php) Test ServicesThe Innovative Engineering Solutions test facility in Murrieta, California provides a wide array of test services to our customers. These include thermal testing, humidity testing, vacuum testing, cryogenic testing, proof pressure and helium leak testing, structural testing, vibration testing, and physical properties testing of ferrous and non-ferrous materials, to name a few. We are familiar with MIL-STD-810 test requirements and comply with ANSI/NCSL Z540.1 and ISO 12075 calibration and accuracy standards for testing laboratories. If your product requires testing, IES can assist you with developmental testing, qualification testing or acceptance testing to meet your needs. Our engineers can also design, develop and build custom data acquisition systems to meet your exacting requirements. [Special Test Equipment](special_test_equipment.php) Special Test EquipmentIES will rapidly design, develop, and fabricate automated test equipment (ATE) and special test equipment (STE) to meet your unique requirements. We have extensive experience in mechanical, electrical, thermal, fluid, and instrumentation and control system designs that meet the needs of the aerospace, medical, and industrial industries. With our in-house machining, fabrication, and assembly capabilities, along with our comprehensive supplier network, we can provide that special test equipment for your development, qualification, or manufacturing needs. We can provide customized data acquisition systems using LabView, and the hardware to make it a turnkey system. Either one piece, or multiple units, IES will work with you to identify the requirements and then develop, build, and deliver that special test equipment to meet your product needs. [Product Development](product_development.php) Product DevelopmentAt Innovative Engineering Solutions we can assist our customers in developing their ideas and requirements into useful products. Over the years our clientele has included large aerospace companies, industrial manufacturers, the medical profession, the agricultural industry, as well as the individual entrepreneurs. We have developed unique aerospace hardware and systems, electronic test equipment, electromechanical devices, automated test equipment, and consumer products. [Engineering Contract Services](contract_services.php) Contract ServicesIf your company has need of short or long term specialty engineering services, IES may have a solution for you. We maintain an extensive network of industry design and analysis engineers, whose specialties include thermal, fluid flow, structural and finite element analysis. We can provide staffing at your facility, or support you off-site. At IES, we have the technical resources to meet your needs. |       Copyright � 2022 IES [Sitemap](sitemap.php)  Powered by San Diego IT Support |

Since 1995, Innovative Engineering Solutions has offered these services to a wide variety of customers, including the aerospace, industrial, commercial, government and medical sectors.  The expertise of Innovative Engineering Solution's engineers include cryogenic test services, aerospace fluid systems, design, analysis, and fabrication of special test equipment, fluid flow and thermal analysis support, CAD/CAM support, cryogenic system design, industrial control systems, commercial product development (electronic and mechanical), and medical device manufacturing support and testing.  A brief list of our services include:  [Engineering Services](design.php) Thermal Analysis and Graphic ImagingIES provides design and analysis support of mechanical, electrical and fluid systems, including structural analysis, thermal analysis and fluid flow analysis.  Our engineers excel at computational fluid dynamics and finite element analysis of components and structures.  Over the years we have designed and developed numerous products for our customers, including industrial control systems for a variety of factory processes.  We also support the aerospace industry with both flight and ground support equipment (GSE), while for the medical field we have developed many medical devices, special test equipment, and automated test equipment to assist with product development and FDA approval. [Test Services](test_facilities.php) Test ServicesThe Innovative Engineering Solutions test facility in Murrieta, California provides a wide array of test services to our customers. These include thermal testing, humidity testing, vacuum testing, cryogenic testing, proof pressure and helium leak testing, structural testing, vibration testing, and physical properties testing of ferrous and non-ferrous materials, to name a few. We are familiar with MIL-STD-810 test requirements and comply with ANSI/NCSL Z540.1 and ISO 12075 calibration and accuracy standards for testing laboratories. If your product requires testing, IES can assist you with developmental testing, qualification testing or acceptance testing to meet your needs. Our engineers can also design, develop and build custom data acquisition systems to meet your exacting requirements. With our in-house machining, fabrication, and assembly capabilities, along with our comprehensive supplier network, we can provide that special test equipment for your development, qualification, or manufacturing needs. We can provide customized data acquisition systems using LabView, and the hardware to make it a turnkey system. Either one piece, or multiple units, IES will work with you to identify the requirements and then develop, build, and deliver that special test equipment to meet your product needs. [Product Development](product_development.php) Product DevelopmentAt Innovative Engineering Solutions we can assist our customers in developing their ideas and requirements into useful products. Over the years our clientele has included large aerospace companies, industrial manufacturers, the medical profession, the agricultural industry, as well as the individual entrepreneurs. We have developed unique aerospace hardware and systems, electronic test equipment, electromechanical devices, automated test equipment, and consumer products. [Engineering Contract Services](contract_services.php) Contract ServicesIf your company has need of short or long term specialty engineering services, IES may have a solution for you. We maintain an extensive network of industry design and analysis engineers, whose specialties include thermal, fluid flow, structural and finite element analysis. We can provide staffing at your facility, or support you off-site. At IES, we have the technical resources to meet your needs. |

# Design, Analysis, Fabrication, Testing

![Innovative Engineering Solutions Murrieta Facility](pictures/ies_facility.gif)**I****nnovative** **E****ngineering** **S****olutions** (IES) is a full-service engineering company, providing “innovative solutions” to unique problems.  At IES, our experienced engineering teams will assist you with the design, analysis, fabrication and testing of your product.

![Innovative Engineering Solutions Murrieta Facility](pictures/ies_facility.gif)

Since 1995, Innovative Engineering Solutions has offered these services to a wide variety of customers, including the aerospace, industrial, commercial, government and medical sectors.  The expertise of Innovative Engineering Solution's engineers include cryogenic test services, aerospace fluid systems, design, analysis, and fabrication of special test equipment, fluid flow and thermal analysis support, CAD/CAM support, cryogenic system design, industrial control systems, commercial product development (electronic and mechanical), and medical device manufacturing support and testing.  A brief list of our services include:

[Engineering Services](design.php)

![Thermal Analysis and Graphic Imaging](pictures/3dflow.gif)IES provides design and analysis support of mechanical, electrical and fluid systems, including structural analysis, thermal analysis and fluid flow analysis.  Our engineers excel at computational fluid dynamics and finite element analysis of components and structures.  Over the years we have designed and developed numerous products for our customers, including industrial control systems for a variety of factory processes.  We also support the aerospace industry with both flight and ground support equipment (GSE), while for the medical field we have developed many medical devices, special test equipment, and automated test equipment to assist with product development and FDA approval.

[Test Services](test_facilities.php)

![Test Services](pictures/vibe_console.gif)The Innovative Engineering Solutions test facility in Murrieta, California provides a wide array of test services to our customers. These include thermal testing, humidity testing, vacuum testing, cryogenic testing, proof pressure and helium leak testing, structural testing, vibration testing, and physical properties testing of ferrous and non-ferrous materials, to name a few. We are familiar with MIL-STD-810 test requirements and comply with ANSI/NCSL Z540.1 and ISO 12075 calibration and accuracy standards for testing laboratories. If your product requires testing, IES can assist you with developmental testing, qualification testing or acceptance testing to meet your needs. Our engineers can also design, develop and build custom data acquisition systems to meet your exacting requirements.

[Special Test Equipment](special_test_equipment.php)

![Special Test Equipment](pictures/test_fixture.gif)IES will rapidly design, develop, and fabricate automated test equipment (ATE) and special test equipment (STE) to meet your unique requirements. We have extensive experience in mechanical, electrical, thermal, fluid, and instrumentation and control system designs that meet the needs of the aerospace, medical, and industrial industries. With our in-house machining, fabrication, and assembly capabilities, along with our comprehensive supplier network, we can provide that special test equipment for your development, qualification, or manufacturing needs. We can provide customized data acquisition systems using LabView, and the hardware to make it a turnkey system. Either one piece, or multiple units, IES will work with you to identify the requirements and then develop, build, and deliver that special test equipment to meet your product needs.

[Product Development](product_development.php)

![Product Development](pictures/cooling_tray.gif)At Innovative Engineering Solutions we can assist our customers in developing their ideas and requirements into useful products. Over the years our clientele has included large aerospace companies, industrial manufacturers, the medical profession, the agricultural industry, as well as the individual entrepreneurs. We have developed unique aerospace hardware and systems, electronic test equipment, electromechanical devices, automated test equipment, and consumer products.

[Engineering Contract Services](contract_services.php)

![Contract Services](pictures/mary_console.gif)If your company has need of short or long term specialty engineering services, IES may have a solution for you. We maintain an extensive network of industry design and analysis engineers, whose specialties include thermal, fluid flow, structural and finite element analysis. We can provide staffing at your facility, or support you off-site. At IES, we have the technical resources to meet your needs.

![Thermal Analysis and Graphic Imaging](pictures/3dflow.gif)IES provides design and analysis support of mechanical, electrical and fluid systems, including structural analysis, thermal analysis and fluid flow analysis.  Our engineers excel at computational fluid dynamics and finite element analysis of components and structures.  Over the years we have designed and developed numerous products for our customers, including industrial control systems for a variety of factory processes.  We also support the aerospace industry with both flight and ground support equipment (GSE), while for the medical field we have developed many medical devices, special test equipment, and automated test equipment to assist with product development and FDA approval.

[Test Services](test_facilities.php)

![Test Services](pictures/vibe_console.gif)The Innovative Engineering Solutions test facility in Murrieta, California provides a wide array of test services to our customers. These include thermal testing, humidity testing, vacuum testing, cryogenic testing, proof pressure and helium leak testing, structural testing, vibration testing, and physical properties testing of ferrous and non-ferrous materials, to name a few. We are familiar with MIL-STD-810 test requirements and comply with ANSI/NCSL Z540.1 and ISO 12075 calibration and accuracy standards for testing laboratories. If your product requires testing, IES can assist you with developmental testing, qualification testing or acceptance testing to meet your needs. Our engineers can also design, develop and build custom data acquisition systems to meet your exacting requirements.

[Special Test Equipment](special_test_equipment.php)

![Special Test Equipment](pictures/test_fixture.gif)IES will rapidly design, develop, and fabricate automated test equipment (ATE) and special test equipment (STE) to meet your unique requirements. We have extensive experience in mechanical, electrical, thermal, fluid, and instrumentation and control system designs that meet the needs of the aerospace, medical, and industrial industries. With our in-house machining, fabrication, and assembly capabilities, along with our comprehensive supplier network, we can provide that special test equipment for your development, qualification, or manufacturing needs. We can provide customized data acquisition systems using LabView, and the hardware to make it a turnkey system. --- SOURCE 3: Aerospace Innovations in Space Travel - South Dakota Mines ---
URL: https://www.sdsmt.edu/academics/academic-departments/mechanical-engineering/aerospace-innovations-in-space-travel.html

SUMMARY:
Discover how aerospace engineers are advancing space travel with new spacecraft designs, propulsion systems, and space colonization plans.

FULL CONTENT:
![](/_resources/assets/icons/sd-logo-sm.svg)
![](/_resources/assets/icons/sd-logo-sm-m.svg)

# Aerospace Innovations in Space Travel

![Biomedical Engineering Students](/academics/assets/Academics.jpg)

Introduction

Space travel is advancing rapidly thanks to the innovative work of aerospace engineers.
These experts are dedicated to crafting the next generation of spacecraft, using cutting-edge
materials and technologies to make journeys safer and more efficient. Their creativity
doesn't stop with the design; it extends to how these vehicles are powered, enabling
missions to venture farther than ever before.

Overcoming the challenges space travel presents, like microgravity and harsh radiation,
is no small feat. But engineers are working tirelessly to find solutions that ensure
both the health of astronauts and the success of their missions. Their work is paving
the way for future space settlements on celestial bodies such as the Moon and Mars.

Students at South Dakota Mines are at the forefront of these developments, participating
in exciting projects that contribute to this thrilling field. By engaging with these
challenges, they help push the boundaries of what’s possible in space exploration,
preparing for a future where living and working in space is a reality.

### Innovations in Spacecraft Design

Spacecraft design has taken a leap forward with the use of new materials and technologies.
Engineers are constantly exploring materials like lightweight composites and advanced
alloys. These materials make spacecraft stronger and lighter, crucial for improving
fuel efficiency and payload capacity. Additionally, technologies such as 3D printing
allow for the rapid production of complex parts, reducing construction time and costs.

One of the major advancements is in spacecraft aerodynamics. Crafting more aerodynamic
shapes reduces drag and improves fuel efficiency. This is vital for ensuring that
rockets can travel longer distances on less fuel, making space missions more sustainable.
Safety is also at the forefront of design innovations. Improved heat shields and fail-safe
systems ensure that spacecraft can withstand the harsh conditions of space travel.

At South Dakota Mines, students contribute to these advancements through hands-on
projects and innovative research. Engaging in design competitions and collaborating
with aerospace companies, they gain real-world experience essential for their future
careers. By working with faculty and industry experts, students help shape the spacecraft
that will explore the cosmos, ready to tackle the most challenging problems in space
engineering.

### Advances in Propulsion Systems

Propulsion systems are the backbone of space travel, and recent developments in this
area promise to revolutionize how far and how fast we can journey through space. Here's
a look at some of the groundbreaking technologies emerging in this field:

The benefits of these systems lie in their ability to reduce travel time and increase
mission range. For example, faster travel reduces the time astronauts are exposed
to cosmic radiation, making missions safer. It also lowers the cost of reaching distant
destinations by improving fuel efficiency.

At South Dakota Mines, students are actively involved in research and development
of these propulsion technologies. Through collaborative projects with NASA and leading
aerospace companies, they participate in creating engines that may power future Mars
missions. By working on cutting-edge propulsion solutions, students not only gain
invaluable experience but also contribute to the forefront of space exploration.

### Overcoming Challenges in Space Travel

Space travel presents many challenges, demanding innovative solutions. Microgravity,
for example, affects the health of astronauts, leading to muscle atrophy and bone
loss. Engineers and scientists are exploring ways to mitigate these effects, such
as specialized exercise regimens and advanced habitat designs that simulate gravity-like
conditions.

Radiation is another significant challenge, as space travelers are exposed to higher
levels of radiation than on Earth. Researchers are developing robust shielding materials
and technologies to protect astronauts. This includes the use of advanced polymers
and metals that can absorb or deflect harmful particles. Collaborating with fields
like biomedical engineering is crucial, as it offers insights into protecting human
health in space.

Interdisciplinary collaboration is essential in tackling these obstacles. Combining
expertise from aerospace engineering, biology, and materials science leads to comprehensive
solutions. At South Dakota Mines, students engage in projects that encourage this
kind of teamwork, preparing them for the multifaceted nature of space challenges.
They get a chance to work on simulations and develop technologies that could one day
be critical in human spaceflight missions.

### The Future of Space Colonization

Space colonization is no longer a distant dream but a tangible goal. The Moon and
Mars stand out as prime candidates for future settlements. These celestial bodies
offer unique opportunities for exploration and research, making them ideal for sustained
human presence. Engineers are busy developing habitats that support life, focusing
on air and water recycling, food production, and waste management systems.

Aerospace engineers are thinking sustainably, planning for a future where humans can
live and thrive off-planet. This involves designing systems that use local resources
rather than relying entirely on supplies from Earth. Technologies like 3D printing
are being explored to build structures using materials found on Mars and the Moon,
reducing the need for heavy cargo launches.

Students at South Dakota Mines have exciting opportunities to be part of space colonization
projects. By engaging in hands-on research and interdisciplinary education, they can
contribute to these groundbreaking efforts. With an emphasis on innovative thinking
and problem-solving, these projects prepare students to be at the forefront of this
new era of space exploration.

### Conclusion

The quest for deeper space exploration is fueled by constant innovation in spacecraft
design, propulsion systems, and overcoming the myriad challenges of a hostile environment.
This journey brings the exciting prospect of space colonization within reach, with
potential homes on the Moon and Mars being more than just theoretical. The work done
by aerospace engineers, often in collaboration with experts in other fields, is paving
the way for these futures.

At South Dakota Mines, students are immersed in an educational environment that fosters
creativity and innovation in aerospace technology. By participating in cutting-edge
research and interdisciplinary collaboration, they are poised to push the boundaries
of what's possible in space travel. If you're inspired to be part of this groundbreaking
field, explore what exciting opportunities are available at South Dakota Mines to
shape the future of space exploration. Learn more about our [aerospace engineering courses](/academics/academic-departments/mechanical-engineering/aerospace_engineering_minor.html).

![South Dakota Mines: An Engineering, Science, and Technology University](/_resources/assets/icons/footer-logo.svg)

--- SOURCE 5: Advanced Engineering Services | Metis Technology Solutions ---
URL: https://www.metis-tech.com/solutions/engineering-services/

SUMMARY:
Metis engineers provide development, assembly, integration and test of ground and flight units, and in-flight operations support on ISS.

FULL CONTENT:
![Metis Header Logo](https://www.metis-tech.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/LogoMetis70x55.png)
![Metis Header Logo](https://www.metis-tech.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/LogoMetis70x55.png)

# Advanced Engineering Services

![Spaceflight Systems](https://www.metis-tech.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/spaceflightsystems2.png "spaceflightsystems2")

## NASA Mission Flight Dynamics

Metis works with NASA to meet the challenges of analyzing and customizing orbit characteristics to mission design requirements, including mission orbit design for science and technical requirements and transfer trajectory design in Earth orbit, lunar, and planetary missions.  
Recent accomplishments include:

## NASA SPHERES and Astrobee Mission

The NASA SPHERES - Synchronized Position Hold, Engage, Reorient, Experimental Satellite - and Astrobees are robotic free-flyers operating aboard the International Space Station. These robots are designed to work autonomously or via remote control by astronauts, flight controllers or researchers on the ground in support of Astronauts' activities. Metis engineers provide development, assembly, integration and test of ground and flight units, and in-flight operations support on ISS, as well as technical support for the [SPHERES](https://www.nasa.gov/spheres/home) ground data system. Not only responsible for hardware prototyping and development, Metis engineers manage the day-to-day activities in the small satellite development laboratory at NASA Ames supplying support and on-hand experience. They recently played lead roles in the replacement of the SPHERES “blue” satellite propulsion system and successful return to flight.

Metis has pioneered the engineering services for Astrobee as lead in the development, assembly, integration and test of ground and flight units. In addition to our management of hardware prototyping and development, our engineers are critical in the developing the mission Safety Data Packages and Flight Certification packages for these ISS missions.

![NASA Starling Mission](https://www.metis-tech.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/11/starling-image.jpg "starling-image")

## NASA Starling Mission

The Starling mission is a NASA Ames-led technology demonstration mission under the Small Spacecraft Technology Program designed to advance the readiness of several swarm-related technologies. Starling consists of four 6U CubeSats planned to launched to LEO in mid-2021. Metis engineers currently support Starling in flight dynamics and mission operations lead roles. As the flight dynamics team lead, Metis manages mission design and orbit determination. We oversee requirements development, modeling of the swarm’s relative motion, and maneuver design. Additionally, we lead the autonomous swarm maintenance experiment that will fly as a software payload. As the Mission Operation Systems (MOS) and Ground Data Systems (GDS) Lead, Metis manages ground data system development, integration, and testing on the GDS side. On the MOS side, we manage the Mission Operation Plan and Mission Operation Test (simulations, rehearsals, End to End Test, NEN Operational readiness test, and other required tests). We also coordinate NEN support, as well as training and certification for the mission operators.

## AFRL R&D INTEGRATED SPACE EXPERIMENTS

Metis supports the Air Force Research Laboratory / Space Vehicles Directorate at the Kirtland Air Force Base in Albuquerque, New Mexico, through all phases of development, from concept; through design, integration, and testing; to launch and mission operations. Metis engineers support development of formation flight requirements, ConOps, and vehicle design, provide integration support for Attitude Determination and Control System (ADCS) subsystems, and develop the code base for attitude control and orbit determination simulations. Metis also performs systems-level cubesat propulsion engineering tasks including requirements development, trade studies, performance evaluations, product selections, and mission concepts.

## 

## NASA LADEE Mission

Metis provided the Flight Dynamics team lead for the detailed mission orbit design on NASA’s LADEE mission. The LADEE lunar orbit called for several years of concentrated orbit design activity to balance science goals, spacecraft capabilities, operational constraints, and the unique, low-altitude gravitational environment. Metis took the lead in the development of customized design tools and ongoing collaboration with science and spacecraft engineering teams.

Metis’ experience with the LADEE project also included:

## STPSat-5 Mission

Metis generates mission operations schedules for rehearsals, simulations and real-time operations, conducts planning using System Tool Kit (STK) to simulate new procedures and rehearse mission operations activities, conducts ground testing with the Multi-Mission Operations Center (MMOC) and the Near Earth Network (NEN) to verify command and telemetry integrity, and creates operations procedures for various stages of flight: launch and early orbit, acquisition and loss of signal timing and delta velocity maneuvers.

![NASA Juno](https://www.metis-tech.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/juno3.png "juno3")

## NASA’s Deep Space Network

Metis provides Network Operations support to [NASA’s DSN](https://deepspace.jpl.nasa.gov/) via technical expertise in network infrastructure, project interface and operations as subcontractor to Peraton Corporation. We also effectuate network operational planning for network preparations, coordination, test and training, formulate Network Operations Plans with guidelines for support of a spacecraft, codify DSN operations functions and interfaces, and detail DSN operational procedures.

Metis additionally supports and sustains engineering of DSN sub-systems. Performing analysis to measure system performance; developing and utilizing metrics to measure and maintain optimum system performance; supplying root cause failure analysis using state of the art techniques; and recommending/implementing sound solutions to minimize equipment downtime and maximize data return.

## NASA Space Network and Near Earth Network

Metis supports NASA’s Space Communications and Navigation Program’s Space Network and Near-Earth Networks, which provide Telemetry, Tracking, and Commanding (TT&C), high rate data downlink, and radiometric tracking services for on-orbit operations. At the Wallops Flight facility in Virginia, Metis software engineers develop and maintain ground systems and scheduling software for NASA Operations, Maintenance and Sustainment tasks. At the White Sands Complex in New Mexico, Metis provides IT system administration, cybersecurity support as well as integration and test of system software upgrades.

![Multi-Mission Operation Center at NASA Ames Research Center](https://www.metis-tech.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/06/unnamed-2-scaled.jpg "Multi-Mission Operation Center at NASA Ames Research Center")

## NASA Ames Multi-Mission Operation Center

Metis personnel currently provide systems administration, systems engineering, network engineering, and software engineering to support the Multi-Mission Operation Center at NASA Ames Research Center. The MMOC provides cost-effective and highly re-configurable/re-useable resources that include facilities, networks, IT equipment, software, and support services to Projects operating at Ames. Metis works closely with customers to determine and communicate needs to the MMOC management to deliver adequate resources for projects. Additionally, we coordinate, plan and schedule changes/upgrades to manage project-critical systems ensuring compliance with NASA requirements. Our efforts cover support to customer and infrastructure projects.

MMOC Customers' Projects include the Starling technology demonstration mission under the Small Spacecraft Technology Program designed to advance the readiness of several swarm-related technologies. Starling consists of four 6U CubeSats planned to launched to LEO in mid-2021. MMOC also provides operational support to the Stratospheric Observatory for Infrared Astronomy installed in a Boeing 747SP aircraft modified to carry a 2.7-meter (106-inch) reflecting telescope, allowing researchers to operate this mobile observation platform observe from almost anywhere in the world, enabling studies of transient events where there are no telescopes.

MMOC Infrastructure Projects are composed of different modules such as network & security, voice system, backup system, inventory system, alert & monitoring system, storage system, change management system, CDM, etc. Metis provides POC/MSEs to each module to help balance the team workload and keep the infrastructure up-to-date and compliant with all NASA requirements critical for MMOC to obtain its yearly authorization to operate to do business at Ames.

## USAF Multi-Mission Satellite Operation Center

Metis’ personnel provided systems administration, systems engineering, network engineering and software engineering to support USAF ground system development and sustaining engineering.

Metis made significant improvements to MMSOC v. 2.0 software architecture, including development of core MPS Command Builder code which needed to be modified to support critical launch activities; development of a modular design for the User Desktop which allows for additional products to be easily added to the drop-down menu; and development of scripts to automate CM processes saving numerous program labor hours.

![Metis Tech gray logo](https://www.metis-tech.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/LogoMetisGray.png "LogoMetisGray")

enriched_points.expanded_content: Engineers are developing advanced materials and technologies to enhance spacecraft design, focusing on performance, efficiency, and safety throughout the mission lifecycle.
Utilizing digital twins and simulation tools, engineers can model spacecraft behavior under various conditions, optimizing designs and improving reliability before actual construction.
Artificial Intelligence aids in data analysis, optimizing mission parameters and automating systems, thus enhancing decision-making and operational efficiency in spacecraft systems.
Modern project management software facilitates better collaboration, tracking, and resource management, allowing teams to adapt quickly to challenges in spacecraft development.
Interagency partnerships foster shared expertise and resources, leading to innovative solutions and enhanced capabilities in spacecraft engineering and mission success.
